title: Convert recursive & explicit forms of arithmetic sequences
description: Given the explicit formula of an arithmetic sequence, find its recursive formula, and vice versa.
topic_path: Convert recursive & explicit forms of arithmetic sequences

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=-11+22(n-1)$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints: From the explicit formula, $\blueD{-11}+\maroonD{22}(n-1)$, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-11}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{22}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$g(n)=1+5(n-1)$

$g(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$g(n)=g(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=41-5n$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$f(\blueD 1)=41-5(\blueD 1)=\blueD{36}$  
$f(\greenD 2)=41-5(\greenD 2)=\greenD{31}$


$f(\greenD 2)-f(\blueD 1)=\greenD{31}-\blueD{36}=\maroonD{-5}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{36}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{-5}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$h(n)=-10+12n$

**Complete the recursive formula of $h(n)$.**

$h(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$h(n)=h(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$h(\blueD 1)=-10+12(\blueD 1)=\blueD{2}$

$h(\greenD 2)=-10+12(\greenD 2)=\greenD{14}$

$h(\greenD 2)-h(\blueD 1)=\greenD{14}-\blueD{2}=\maroonD{12}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{2}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{12}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$g(n)=-50-15n$

**Complete the recursive formula of $g(n)$.**

$g(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$g(n)=g(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$g(\blueD 1)=-50-15(\blueD 1)=\blueD{-65}$

$g(\greenD 2)=-50-15(\greenD 2)=\greenD{-80}$

$g(\greenD 2)-g(\blueD 1)=\greenD{-80}-(\blueD{-65})=\maroonD{-15}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-65}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{-15}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$f(n)=64+6n$

**Complete the recursive formula of $f(n)$.**

$f(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$f(n)=f(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$f(\blueD 1)=64+6(\blueD 1)=\blueD{70}$

$f(\greenD 2)=64+6(\greenD 2)=\greenD{76}$

$f(\greenD 2)-f(\blueD 1)=\greenD{76}-\blueD{70}=\maroonD{6}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{70}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{6}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$h(n)=-13n$

**Complete the recursive formula of $h(n)$.**

$h(1)=$ [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

$h(n)=h(n-1)+$ [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
$h(\blueD 1)=-13(\blueD 1)=\blueD{-13}$

$h(\greenD 2)=-13(\greenD 2)=\greenD{-26}$

$h(\greenD 2)-h(\blueD 1)=\greenD{-26}-(\blueD{-13})=\maroonD{-13}$

So the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-13}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{-13}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}
f(1)=-8
\\\\
f(n)=f(n-1)-3
\end{cases}$

**Find an explicit formula for $f(n)$.**

$f(n)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: From the recursive formula, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{-8}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{-3}$.

This is the explicit formula of the sequence:

$f(n)=\blueD{-8} \maroonD{-3}(n-1)$

*Note that this solution strategy results in this formula, however an equally correct solution can be written in other equivalent forms as well.*

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}
g(1)=51
\\\\
g(n)=g(n-1)+2
\end{cases}$

$g(n)=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: From the recursive formula, we can tell that the first term of the sequence is $\blueD{51}$ and the common difference is $\maroonD{2}$.

This is the explicit formula of the sequence:

$g(n)=\blueD{51} +\maroonD{2}(n-1)$

*Note that this solution strategy results in this formula, however an equally correct solution can be written in other equivalent forms as well.*
